If a love for life leads us to earnestly plead with those who can only kill the body, how fervent should our prayers be to Him, who is able to destroy both body and soul in hell! How should we pray for the salvation of our relatives, friends, and all around us! When we petition great men, we must be cautious not to offend them; even just complaints must often be held back. But when we approach the King of kings with reverence, we cannot ask or expect too much. Though nothing but wrath is what we deserve, God is able and willing to do far more than all we can ask or think.

The king was angry: those who do things on their own initiative later reflect upon them with regret. When angry, we should pause before making any decisions, and thus control our own spirits, and show that we are governed by reason. Those who are most arrogant and insolent when in power and prosperity commonly, like Haman, are the most dejected and spiritless when brought down. The day is coming when those who hate and persecute God’s chosen ones would gladly be indebted to them. The king returned even more angry against Haman. Those around him were ready to carry out his anger. How little can proud men be sure of the advantage they think they have! The enemies of God's church have often been trapped in their own cleverness. The Lord is known by such judgments. Then the king’s wrath was calmed, and not until then. And who pities Haman hanged on his own gallows? Who does not rather rejoice in the divine righteousness displayed in the destruction his own schemes brought upon him? Let those who do evil tremble, turn to the Lord, and seek forgiveness through the blood of Jesus.
